title: F002: Add Keystone reconciler integration tests
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** backend
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** CC-0014 (S014 in PLAN.md)

## Summary

Add envtest integration tests (CC-0014) for the Keystone reconciler exercising the full reconciliation loop against a real API server with etcd. Tests create prerequisite Secrets simulating ESO sync, create a Keystone CR, then drive condition progression through SecretsReady → FernetKeysReady → (ConfigMap created) → DatabaseReady → DeploymentReady → BootstrapReady → Ready by simulating external dependencies (MariaDB readiness, Job completion, Deployment availability). They verify correct creation of all child resources (Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Jobs, CronJob, Secret, RBAC, PushSecret) and that `status.endpoint` and `status.conditions` (including ObservedGeneration) are populated correctly. Both brownfield and managed database modes are covered.

## Scope

**Included:**
- New integration test file `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go` with `//go:build integration` tag
- Full reconciliation loop test (brownfield mode): create prereqs → create CR → manager-driven reconcile → verify all 6 conditions reach True
- Full reconciliation loop test (managed mode): same flow but with `spec.database.clusterRef` + MariaDB CR simulation via `SimulateDatabaseReady`, `SimulateUserReady`, `SimulateGrantReady`
- Condition progression test: verify each sub-condition transitions False→True in reconciliation order, later conditions remain absent/False until predecessors are satisfied
- Resource creation verification: assert Deployment (`{name}-api`), Service (`{name}-api`), ConfigMap (immutable, `{name}-config-{hash}`), Jobs (`{name}-db-sync`, `{name}-bootstrap`), CronJob (`{name}-fernet-rotate`), Secret (`{name}-fernet-keys`), ServiceAccount (`{name}-fernet-rotate`), Role (`{name}-fernet-rotate`), RoleBinding (`{name}-fernet-rotate`), PushSecret (`{name}-fernet-keys-backup`) exist after full reconciliation
- Status field verification: `status.endpoint` matches `http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3`, `status.conditions` ObservedGeneration matches CR Generation
- `SimulateDeploymentReady` function in shared simulators package (`internal/common/testutil/simulators/simulators.go`)
- New `SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController` helper in `operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go` that registers the `KeystoneReconciler` via `SetupWithManager` alongside CRDs and webhooks; must also install fake CRDs for MariaDB, ESO, and cert-manager types from the common envtest `fake_crds/` directory and register them in the scheme (via `SharedScheme()` or equivalent)
- Build tag `integration` consistent with existing CC-0012 tests

**Excluded:**
- Webhook defaulting/validation testing (already covered by CC-0012 — YAGNI)
- Error injection / fault tolerance tests (separate feature, not requested)
- Chainsaw E2E tests (deferred to Phase 7 per PLAN.md)
- Federation, middleware, plugin, or policyOverrides paths (not part of core reconciliation loop, no current requirement)
- Performance / stress testing (YAGNI)
- Deletion / finalizer testing (not implemented in the reconciler, YAGNI)
- `test-integration` Makefile target wiring (currently a stub, separate infrastructure concern)

## Visualization

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant T as Test
    participant API as envtest API Server
    participant R as Keystone Reconciler
    participant S as Simulators

    T->>API: Create Namespace
    T->>API: Create ExternalSecrets
    T->>API: Create Secrets (DB creds, admin creds)
    S->>API: SimulateExternalSecretSync
    T->>API: Create Keystone CR
    Note over R: Manager triggers reconcile
    R->>API: Check ExternalSecret + Secret
    R->>API: Set SecretsReady=True
    R->>API: Create Fernet Keys Secret
    R->>API: Create SA, Role, RoleBinding
    R->>API: Create CronJob, PushSecret
    R->>API: Set FernetKeysReady=True
    R->>API: Create ConfigMap (immutable, hashed)
    R->>API: Create db-sync Job
    R->>API: Set DatabaseReady=False (DBSyncInProgress)
    S->>API: SimulateJobComplete (db-sync)
    Note over R: Requeue triggers reconcile
    R->>API: Set DatabaseReady=True
    R->>API: Create Deployment + Service
    R->>API: Set DeploymentReady=False
    S->>API: SimulateDeploymentReady
    Note over R: Requeue triggers reconcile
    R->>API: Set DeploymentReady=True, status.endpoint
    R->>API: Create bootstrap Job
    R->>API: Set BootstrapReady=False
    S->>API: SimulateJobComplete (bootstrap)
    Note over R: Requeue triggers reconcile
    R->>API: Set BootstrapReady=True
    R->>API: Set Ready=True (AllReady)
    T->>API: Verify all conditions, resources, status
```

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> SecretsReady: ESO Secrets exist
    SecretsReady --> FernetKeysReady: Fernet Secret + RBAC + CronJob + PushSecret created
    FernetKeysReady --> DatabaseReady: ConfigMap created, db-sync Job completes
    DatabaseReady --> DeploymentReady: Deployment + Service created, pods available
    DeploymentReady --> BootstrapReady: Bootstrap Job completes
    BootstrapReady --> Ready: AllTrue aggregation
    Ready --> [*]
```

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph TestFile["integration_test.go"]
        TFL["TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Brownfield"]
        TFM["TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Managed"]
        TCP["TestIntegration_ConditionProgression"]
        TRC["TestIntegration_ResourceCreation"]
        TSE["TestIntegration_StatusEndpoint"]
        TOG["TestIntegration_ObservedGeneration"]
    end
    subgraph Helpers["testutil/envtest_setup.go"]
        SK["SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController (new)"]
        SE2["SetupKeystoneEnvTest (unchanged)"]
    end
    subgraph Simulators["simulators/simulators.go"]
        SD["SimulateDeploymentReady (new)"]
        SE["SimulateExternalSecretSync"]
        SJ["SimulateJobComplete"]
        SDB["SimulateDatabaseReady"]
        SU["SimulateUserReady"]
        SG["SimulateGrantReady"]
    end
    TestFile --> SK
    TestFile --> Simulators
```

## Key Components

- **`operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go`** (new): Main test file with `//go:build integration` tag. Contains 6 test functions using `testing.T` + gomega (dot-imported), consistent with CC-0012 patterns. Each test calls `testutil.SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t)`, creates an isolated namespace, uses `gomega.Eventually` for polling condition progression. Brownfield test uses `spec.database.host`; managed test uses `spec.database.clusterRef` and exercises MariaDB CR creation + simulation.
- **`operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go`** (modified): New `SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController(t, addToScheme, registerWebhooks, registerController)` function that extends envtest setup to also register the `KeystoneReconciler` via `SetupWithManager`. Must install additional fake CRDs (MariaDB Database/User/Grant, ESO ExternalSecret/PushSecret) alongside the Keystone CRD, and register their types in the scheme. Existing `SetupKeystoneEnvTest` remains unchanged — CC-0012 webhook tests are unaffected.
- **`internal/common/testutil/simulators/simulators.go`** (modified): New `SimulateDeploymentReady(ctx, c, key, replicas)` function that sets `status.conditions[Available]=True`, `status.readyReplicas`, and `status.observedGeneration` on an existing Deployment. Follows the established typed-resource simulator pattern (like `SimulateMariaDBReady`).
- **Existing simulators** (unchanged, consumed): `SimulateExternalSecretSync`, `SimulateJobComplete`, `SimulateDatabaseReady`, `SimulateUserReady`, `SimulateGrantReady` — simulate external controller behavior in envtest where those controllers don't run.
- **Existing `builders.SecretBuilder`** (unchanged, consumed): Used to construct prerequisite DB credentials Secret (keys: `username`, `password`) and admin credentials Secret (key: `password`).

stories.title: Platform engineer verifies brownfield Keystone reconciliation loop
stories.want: to run integration tests that exercise the full Keystone reconciliation loop in brownfield mode (external database host)
stories.so_that: I have confidence that the reconciler creates all expected child resources and progresses through all conditions to Ready=True when using a pre-existing database
criteria: Test creates prerequisite Secrets (DB credentials with username+password, admin credentials with password) and ExternalSecrets, then creates a Keystone CR with spec.database.host set

After simulating external dependencies (ExternalSecret sync, Job completion, Deployment readiness), all 6 conditions reach True: SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, Ready
status.endpoint is set to http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3
All condition ObservedGeneration values match the Keystone CR's metadata.generation

stories.title: Platform engineer verifies managed-database Keystone reconciliation loop
stories.want: to run integration tests that exercise the full Keystone reconciliation loop in managed mode (spec.database.clusterRef)
stories.so_that: I have confidence that the reconciler correctly creates MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs and waits for their readiness before proceeding
criteria: Test creates a Keystone CR with spec.database.clusterRef pointing to a MariaDB instance
Reconciler creates MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs owned by the Keystone CR
After simulating MariaDB readiness (SimulateDatabaseReady, SimulateUserReady, SimulateGrantReady) and other dependencies, all 6 conditions reach True
The same child resources are created as in brownfield mode (Deployment, Service, ConfigMap, Jobs, CronJob, Secret, RBAC, PushSecret)

stories.title: Platform engineer verifies condition progression order
stories.want: to verify that Keystone conditions transition in the correct order and later conditions remain absent or False until predecessors are satisfied
stories.so_that: I can trust that the reconciler enforces sequential dependency ordering and doesn't skip ahead
criteria: Before secrets are ready, only SecretsReady condition exists (False); FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady are absent
After SecretsReady=True, FernetKeysReady transitions to True; DatabaseReady remains False (DBSyncInProgress) until db-sync Job completes
After DatabaseReady=True, DeploymentReady transitions to False (WaitingForDeployment) until Deployment is available
After DeploymentReady=True, BootstrapReady transitions to False (BootstrapInProgress) until bootstrap Job completes; Ready=True only after all sub-conditions are True

stories.title: Platform engineer verifies all child resources are created
stories.want: to verify that all expected child resources exist after a full reconciliation
stories.so_that: I can trust that the reconciler creates the complete set of Kubernetes resources needed to run Keystone
criteria: Deployment {name}-api, Service {name}-api, and immutable ConfigMap {name}-config-{hash} exist in the namespace
Jobs {name}-db-sync and {name}-bootstrap exist
CronJob {name}-fernet-rotate, Secret {name}-fernet-keys, ServiceAccount {name}-fernet-rotate, Role {name}-fernet-rotate, RoleBinding {name}-fernet-rotate exist
PushSecret {name}-fernet-keys-backup exists

stories.title: Test infrastructure provides SimulateDeploymentReady for envtest
stories.want: a SimulateDeploymentReady simulator function that makes a Deployment appear ready in envtest
stories.so_that: integration tests for any operator can simulate Deployment readiness without running the real deployment controller
criteria: SimulateDeploymentReady(ctx, c, key, replicas) sets status.conditions with Available=True, status.readyReplicas to the given count, and status.observedGeneration to match the Deployment's metadata.generation
The function follows the existing typed-resource simulator pattern (Get → modify status → Status().Update)
IsDeploymentReady from the deployment package returns true after SimulateDeploymentReady is called
A unit test in simulators package verifies the function works correctly against the fake client

stories.title: Test infrastructure provides SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController
stories.want: a SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController helper that starts an envtest environment with the KeystoneReconciler registered
stories.so_that: integration tests can exercise the full reconciliation loop with a real API server instead of unit-testing individual sub-reconcilers
criteria: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController installs Keystone CRD, webhook configs, and fake CRDs (MariaDB, ESO, cert-manager) from the common fake_crds directory
The controller-runtime Manager starts with the KeystoneReconciler registered via SetupWithManager
All required types (Keystone, MariaDB, ESO, cert-manager, core K8s) are registered in the scheme
Existing SetupKeystoneEnvTest function remains unchanged — CC-0012 webhook tests are not affected

requirements.description: SimulateDeploymentReady SHALL set status.conditions with Available=True, status.readyReplicas, and status.observedGeneration on an existing Deployment
requirements.rationale: The deployment common package's IsDeploymentReady checks all three fields; the simulator must satisfy all checks to be useful in integration tests

scenarios.name: Deployment becomes ready
scenarios.when: SimulateDeploymentReady is called with a valid Deployment key and replicas=3
scenarios.then: the Deployment's status.conditions contains Available=True, status.readyReplicas=3, and status.observedGeneration matches metadata.generation
and_then: IsDeploymentReady returns true for the updated Deployment

scenarios.name: Deployment not found returns error
scenarios.when: SimulateDeploymentReady is called with a key that does not exist
scenarios.then: a wrapped NotFound error is returned
and_then: the error message includes the Deployment key

scenarios.name: Simulator is idempotent
scenarios.when: SimulateDeploymentReady is called twice on the same Deployment
scenarios.then: the second call succeeds without error and the Deployment remains ready

requirements.description: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController SHALL start an envtest environment with the KeystoneReconciler registered via SetupWithManager, installing Keystone CRD, webhook configs, and all required fake CRDs
requirements.rationale: Integration tests need a running manager with the reconciler to test the full reconciliation loop; fake CRDs are needed because the reconciler creates MariaDB, ESO, and cert-manager resources

scenarios.name: Environment starts successfully
scenarios.when: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController is called with valid addToScheme, registerWebhooks, and registerController functions
scenarios.then: it returns a client, context, and cancel function; the manager is running with the KeystoneReconciler
and_then: creating a Keystone CR triggers the reconciler

scenarios.name: Fake CRDs are installed
scenarios.when: the envtest environment is started
scenarios.then: MariaDB Database/User/Grant, ESO ExternalSecret/PushSecret, and cert-manager Certificate CRDs are available in the API server
and_then: resources of these types can be created and queried

scenarios.name: Scheme includes all required types
scenarios.when: the envtest environment is started
scenarios.then: the scheme includes Keystone, MariaDB, ESO v1beta1, ESO v1alpha1, cert-manager, and core K8s types
and_then: typed clients can get/list/update resources of all these types

requirements.description: The brownfield full-reconcile integration test SHALL verify that all 6 conditions (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady, Ready) reach True after simulating all external dependencies
requirements.rationale: This is the primary happy-path test verifying the complete reconciliation loop works end-to-end in brownfield mode

scenarios.name: All conditions reach True
scenarios.when: prerequisite Secrets and ExternalSecrets are created, a brownfield Keystone CR is created, and external dependencies are simulated (ExternalSecret sync, Job completion, Deployment readiness)
scenarios.then: all 6 conditions on the Keystone CR reach status True

scenarios.name: Status endpoint is populated
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes
scenarios.then: status.endpoint equals http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3

scenarios.name: Conditions have correct ObservedGeneration
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes
scenarios.then: all conditions have ObservedGeneration matching the Keystone CR's metadata.generation

requirements.description: The managed-mode full-reconcile integration test SHALL verify that MariaDB Database/User/Grant CRs are created and that all 6 conditions reach True after simulating MariaDB readiness
requirements.rationale: Managed mode is a distinct code path where the reconciler creates MariaDB CRs instead of using an external host; both modes must be tested

scenarios.name: MariaDB CRs are created
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with spec.database.clusterRef is created and prerequisites are satisfied
scenarios.then: MariaDB Database, User, and Grant CRs are created in the same namespace
and_then: the CRs reference the correct MariaDB cluster

scenarios.name: All conditions reach True after MariaDB simulation
scenarios.when: SimulateDatabaseReady, SimulateUserReady, SimulateGrantReady are called for the MariaDB CRs, and other dependencies are simulated
scenarios.then: all 6 conditions on the Keystone CR reach status True

scenarios.name: DatabaseReady waits for MariaDB CRs
scenarios.when: MariaDB CRs exist but have not been simulated as ready
scenarios.then: DatabaseReady condition is False with reason WaitingForDatabase

requirements.description: The resource creation integration test SHALL verify that all expected child resources exist after a full reconciliation
requirements.rationale: The reconciler creates 12+ child resources; verifying their existence ensures no resource creation was accidentally omitted or broken

scenarios.name: Core workload resources exist
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes in brownfield mode
scenarios.then: Deployment {name}-api, Service {name}-api, and ConfigMap {name}-config-{hash} exist in the namespace

scenarios.name: Job resources exist
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes
scenarios.then: Jobs {name}-db-sync and {name}-bootstrap exist in the namespace

scenarios.name: Fernet rotation resources exist
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes
scenarios.then: CronJob {name}-fernet-rotate, Secret {name}-fernet-keys, ServiceAccount {name}-fernet-rotate, Role {name}-fernet-rotate, RoleBinding {name}-fernet-rotate, and PushSecret {name}-fernet-keys-backup exist

requirements.description: The status endpoint integration test SHALL verify that status.endpoint is set to the correct URL format after DeploymentReady=True
requirements.rationale: Downstream consumers depend on status.endpoint to discover the Keystone API; an incorrect URL would break service discovery

scenarios.name: Endpoint format is correct
scenarios.when: the DeploymentReady condition reaches True
scenarios.then: status.endpoint equals http://{name}-api.{namespace}.svc.cluster.local:5000/v3

scenarios.name: Endpoint is empty before DeploymentReady
scenarios.when: the reconciler has not yet reached the deployment phase
scenarios.then: status.endpoint is empty string

requirements.description: The ObservedGeneration integration test SHALL verify that all conditions have ObservedGeneration matching the Keystone CR's metadata.generation
requirements.rationale: ObservedGeneration indicates whether the controller has processed the latest spec; stale values would mislead operators about reconciliation progress

scenarios.name: ObservedGeneration matches after initial reconciliation
scenarios.when: the full reconciliation completes on a newly created Keystone CR
scenarios.then: all conditions have ObservedGeneration equal to the CR's metadata.generation

scenarios.name: ObservedGeneration updates after spec change
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR spec is updated (generation incremented) and reconciliation completes
scenarios.then: all conditions have ObservedGeneration equal to the new metadata.generation

requirements.description: The condition progression integration test SHALL verify that conditions transition in the defined order: SecretsReady → FernetKeysReady → DatabaseReady → DeploymentReady → BootstrapReady → Ready
requirements.rationale: The reconciler processes sub-reconcilers sequentially and returns early when any is not ready; tests must verify this ordering is enforced

scenarios.name: Early conditions set before later ones
scenarios.when: secrets are ready but db-sync Job has not completed
scenarios.then: SecretsReady=True and FernetKeysReady=True, but DatabaseReady=False with reason DBSyncInProgress; DeploymentReady and BootstrapReady are absent or False

scenarios.name: Ready is False until all sub-conditions are True
scenarios.when: all conditions except BootstrapReady are True
scenarios.then: Ready=False with reason NotAllReady
and_then: Ready transitions to True with reason AllReady only after BootstrapReady=True

scenarios.name: SecretsReady blocks all subsequent conditions
scenarios.when: ExternalSecrets exist but Secrets are not yet synced
scenarios.then: SecretsReady=False; no other conditions are set

requirements.description: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController SHALL NOT modify the existing SetupKeystoneEnvTest function or its behavior
requirements.rationale: CC-0012 webhook integration tests depend on SetupKeystoneEnvTest; changing it could break existing tests

scenarios.name: Existing function unchanged
scenarios.when: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController is added to envtest_setup.go
scenarios.then: SetupKeystoneEnvTest retains its original signature and behavior
and_then: CC-0012 integration tests continue to pass without modification

scenarios.name: Shared code extracted if beneficial
scenarios.when: SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController shares setup logic with SetupKeystoneEnvTest
scenarios.then: common logic MAY be extracted to unexported helpers, but SetupKeystoneEnvTest's public API and behavior remain identical

tasks.title: Add SimulateDeploymentReady to shared simulators package with unit test (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Add SimulateDeploymentReady(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, key client.ObjectKey, replicas int32) error to internal/common/testutil/simulators/simulators.go. The function gets the existing Deployment, sets status.conditions with DeploymentAvailable=True, status.readyReplicas=replicas, status.observedGeneration=deploy.Generation, then calls c.Status().Update(). Follow the typed-resource pattern used by SimulateJobComplete. Add a unit test in simulators_test.go (non-integration) using the fake client that verifies: (a) readyReplicas is set, (b) Available condition is True, (c) observedGeneration matches generation, (d) IsDeploymentReady returns true after the call. Feature comment: // Feature: CC-0014.

tasks.title: Add SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController to envtest_setup.go (REQ-002, REQ-009)
tasks.description: Add SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController(t testing.TB, addToScheme func(*runtime.Scheme) error, registerWebhooks func(ctrl.Manager) error, registerController func(ctrl.Manager) error) (client.Client, context.Context, context.CancelFunc) to operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go. This function extends the existing SetupKeystoneEnvTest pattern to additionally: (1) install fake CRDs from internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/ (mariadb-operator/*.yaml, external-secrets/*.yaml, cert-manager/*.yaml) alongside the Keystone CRD, (2) build a scheme that includes Keystone types plus SharedScheme() types (MariaDB, ESO, cert-manager, core K8s), (3) call registerController(mgr) after starting the manager to register the KeystoneReconciler. Use runtime.Caller(0) relative paths to locate fake_crds/. Leave SetupKeystoneEnvTest completely unchanged. Extract shared setup logic into unexported helpers if it reduces duplication without affecting SetupKeystoneEnvTest's behavior.

tasks.title: Create integration_test.go with shared helpers and TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Brownfield (REQ-003, REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go with //go:build integration tag. Define shared helpers: (a) setupEnvTestWithController(t) calling testutil.SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController with keystonev1alpha1.AddToScheme, KeystoneWebhook.SetupWebhookWithManager, and KeystoneReconciler.SetupWithManager; (b) createPrerequisites(t, ctx, c, ns, ks) that creates ExternalSecrets (db, admin), Secrets with correct keys (using builders.SecretBuilder), and calls SimulateExternalSecretSync; (c) driveFullReconciliation(t, ctx, c, ns, ks) that polls for condition progression and calls SimulateJobComplete (db-sync), SimulateDeploymentReady, SimulateJobComplete (bootstrap) at appropriate points. Write TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Brownfield: creates namespace (GenerateName), creates brownfield Keystone CR (spec.database.host), calls createPrerequisites and driveFullReconciliation, then asserts: all 6 conditions are True, status.endpoint is correct, ObservedGeneration matches. Use gomega dot-import and EventuallyCondition from assertions package.

tasks.title: Add TestIntegration_ConditionProgression test (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Add TestIntegration_ConditionProgression to integration_test.go. This test creates a Keystone CR in brownfield mode but drives the reconciliation step-by-step, pausing between phases to verify intermediate condition states. Steps: (1) Create namespace, ExternalSecrets, Secrets but do NOT call SimulateExternalSecretSync yet. Wait for SecretsReady=False (WaitingForDBCredentials). Verify FernetKeysReady/DatabaseReady/DeploymentReady/BootstrapReady are absent. (2) Call SimulateExternalSecretSync for both ExternalSecrets. Wait for SecretsReady=True, then FernetKeysReady=True. Verify DatabaseReady=False (DBSyncInProgress). (3) Call SimulateJobComplete for db-sync. Wait for DatabaseReady=True. Verify DeploymentReady=False (WaitingForDeployment). (4) Call SimulateDeploymentReady. Wait for DeploymentReady=True. Verify BootstrapReady=False (BootstrapInProgress). (5) Call SimulateJobComplete for bootstrap. Wait for BootstrapReady=True, Ready=True (AllReady). Use EventuallyCondition with appropriate timeouts.

tasks.title: Add TestIntegration_ResourceCreation, TestIntegration_StatusEndpoint, TestIntegration_ObservedGeneration tests (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Add three focused tests to integration_test.go: (1) TestIntegration_ResourceCreation: runs full brownfield reconciliation, then uses AssertResourceExists to verify all 12 child resources exist: Deployment test-keystone-api, Service test-keystone-api, ConfigMap matching test-keystone-config-* (use client.List with label/prefix matching), Jobs test-keystone-db-sync and test-keystone-bootstrap, CronJob test-keystone-fernet-rotate, Secret test-keystone-fernet-keys, ServiceAccount test-keystone-fernet-rotate, Role test-keystone-fernet-rotate, RoleBinding test-keystone-fernet-rotate, PushSecret test-keystone-fernet-keys-backup. (2) TestIntegration_StatusEndpoint: runs full reconciliation, verifies status.endpoint matches expected format. (3) TestIntegration_ObservedGeneration: runs full reconciliation, fetches Keystone CR, iterates all conditions verifying each has ObservedGeneration == ks.Generation.

tasks.title: Add TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Managed test (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add TestIntegration_FullReconcile_Managed to integration_test.go. Creates a Keystone CR with spec.database.clusterRef (pointing to a MariaDB cluster name) instead of spec.database.host. Creates prerequisites (ExternalSecrets, Secrets, simulate sync). The reconciler will create MariaDB Database, User, Grant CRs. The test uses gomega.Eventually to wait for these CRs to appear, then calls SimulateDatabaseReady, SimulateUserReady, SimulateGrantReady. Then continues with SimulateJobComplete (db-sync), SimulateDeploymentReady, SimulateJobComplete (bootstrap). Verifies all 6 conditions reach True, status.endpoint is correct. Also verifies MariaDB CRs exist with correct names (same as Keystone CR name).

review_criteria: All 6 integration test functions pass with `go test -tags=integration` against envtest (requires KUBEBUILDER_ASSETS)
SimulateDeploymentReady unit test passes and deployment.IsDeploymentReady returns true after simulation
SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController correctly installs all fake CRDs (MariaDB, ESO, cert-manager) and starts the manager with the reconciler
Existing CC-0012 webhook integration tests (operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go) continue to pass unchanged
All test functions follow established patterns: SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable, GenerateName namespaces, gomega dot-import, EventuallyCondition for polling
Code passes golangci-lint and go vet without errors
Both brownfield (spec.database.host) and managed (spec.database.clusterRef) database modes are tested end-to-end
Condition progression test verifies intermediate states, not just final state — each phase is checked before advancing
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE: The integration tests exercise the full reconciliation loop by running the KeystoneReconciler inside a controller-runtime Manager against envtest's real API server with etcd. External controllers (ESO operator, MariaDB operator, Kubernetes deployment controller) don't run in envtest, so their behavior is simulated using the shared simulator functions.

KEY PATTERN - ENVTEST WITH CONTROLLER: The new SetupKeystoneEnvTestWithController extends the existing SetupKeystoneEnvTest pattern (operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go) by adding: (1) fake CRD installation from internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/, (2) registerController callback for SetupWithManager. It reuses the same manager lifecycle (goroutine, cleanup via t.Cleanup) and direct-client creation. Fake CRD paths are resolved relative to the source file using runtime.Caller(0) — navigate up to the repo root, then into internal/common/testutil/fake_crds/.

KEY PATTERN - SIMULATOR: SimulateDeploymentReady follows the typed-resource pattern (like SimulateJobComplete): Get the existing object, modify status fields, call c.Status().Update(). It must set three things to satisfy IsDeploymentReady: (a) status.conditions with DeploymentAvailable=True, (b) status.readyReplicas >= spec.replicas, (c) status.observedGeneration >= metadata.generation.

KEY PATTERN - TEST STRUCTURE: Each test follows: (1) SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable, (2) create unique namespace with GenerateName, (3) create prerequisites (ExternalSecrets, Secrets, simulate sync), (4) create Keystone CR, (5) poll for conditions using EventuallyCondition, (6) simulate external dependencies at appropriate points. The driveFullReconciliation helper encapsulates the simulation loop.

PITFALLS: (1) ConfigMap names include a content hash — use client.List with label selector or prefix matching to find them. (2) The reconciler creates fernet keys on first reconcile then requeues — tests must account for this extra reconcile cycle. (3) Jobs use PodSpecHash annotation for spec-change detection — simulator doesn't need to set this as the integration test uses real reconciler-created Jobs. (4) The managed-mode test must create the Keystone CR with clusterRef and without host (CEL validation enforces mutual exclusivity).

FILES TO CREATE: operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go (new, ~400 lines).
FILES TO MODIFY: internal/common/testutil/simulators/simulators.go (add ~25 lines), internal/common/testutil/simulators/simulators_test.go (add ~40 lines), operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go (add ~80 lines).
FILES UNCHANGED: operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/integration_test.go (CC-0012), all sub-reconciler files, all existing simulators.
